description: Temporary task board for Stripe billing, credit integrity, and release verification improvements. Delete this file after all completion criteria are met.
completionCriteria: All P0/P1 implementation and verification tasks are Done.
Stripe Test Mode checkout, portal plan change, cancellation, webhook, notification, and credit reconciliation paths are verified or explicitly blocked with evidence.
Local focused tests and migration safety checks pass.
Staging verification passes before production promotion.
Production deploy and post-deploy health checks complete, or deployment is blocked by an explicit release risk accepted outside this temporary task board.
This temporary JSON file is deleted after completion.

tasks.task: Create and maintain the temporary billing Stripe task list.
acceptanceCriteria: Task JSON exists before implementation and is updated as work progresses.

tasks.task: Add additive schema support for Stripe event ordering, billing side-effect idempotency, and typed credit transaction state.
acceptanceCriteria: subscriptions can record the latest Stripe event timestamp/id.
credit_transactions records status/idempotency metadata without relying on description markers.
billing notifications can be deduplicated by source event.

tasks.task: Harden Checkout and Webhook reconciliation against duplicate customers, non-terminal subscriptions, ownership mismatch, and out-of-order events.
acceptanceCriteria: Checkout refuses non-terminal existing subscriptions and persists new customer ids promptly.
Webhook validates customer/subscription/user ownership before entitlement changes.
Older Stripe events cannot overwrite newer entitlement state.

tasks.task: Make credit reservation and monthly grant state explicit and idempotent.
acceptanceCriteria: Reservation confirm/cancel uses status columns and remains safe on duplicate calls.
Monthly grant ledger cannot duplicate under concurrent reset attempts.

tasks.task: Adjust billing return UX so Stripe Portal returns do not imply webhook-confirmed success prematurely.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings and pricing flows show user-facing copy consistent with asynchronous Stripe updates.

tasks.task: Update billing and Stripe operations docs to match the implemented reconciliation model.
acceptanceCriteria: Docs describe non-terminal subscription handling, webhook ordering, credit diff allocation, and test-mode verification.

tasks.task: Run local focused tests, Stripe Test Mode verification, staging verification, and production release checks.
acceptanceCriteria: Stripe Test Mode scenarios pass or are blocked by missing provider credentials with evidence.
Production deployment and health checks complete or are explicitly blocked by release scope risk.
evidence: PASS: npm run test:unit -- src/app/api/stripe/checkout/route.test.ts src/app/api/webhooks/stripe/route.test.ts src/lib/credits/monthly-reset.test.ts src/lib/credits/reservations.test.ts src/lib/notifications.test.ts (5 files, 67 tests)
PASS: blocking code-reviewer re-review found no remaining high/medium billing correctness issues.
PASS_WITH_WARNINGS: bash scripts/ci/validate-migrations.sh (0037 data-write and non-concurrent index warnings require DBA/release review)

PASS: env BETTER_AUTH_SECRET=test-secret-at-least-32-characters DATABASE_URL=postgresql://test:test@localhost:5432/test GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=test-client-id GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=test-client-secret BETTER_AUTH_URL=http://localhost:3000 npm run test:release-critical (15 release-critical Vitest files, 104 tests)
PASS_WITH_MANUAL_REVIEW: make stripe-preflight (live Products/Webhook/Portal/Account OK; Commerce Disclosure URL requires Dashboard manual check)
BLOCKED: make test-e2e-functional-local-billing failed before app startup because .env.local line 25 is not shell-sourceable (project-ref parsed as command).
BLOCKED: make ops-release-check failed because VERCEL_PROJECT_ID is missing in career-compass-bundle-vercel-staging.vmw2Id after provider auth checks.
BLOCKED: staging/production deployment not attempted because the repository has a large pre-existing dirty worktree across unrelated domains, making release scope isolation unsafe.
